bonuses.leadin: Gaspar Ofhuys’ account of this painter's mental breakdown in which he thought his soul was lost inspired an Emile Wauters painting in which he sits on a monastery chair. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter and resident of the Red Cloisters, who painted a work whose off-kilter shepherds inspired Domenico Ghirlandaio’s “Adoration of the Shepherds.”

The Portinari altarpiece was the first oil on panel work in Florence, beating out Hans Memling’s stolen painting of this scene. Daniele da Voltera earned the nickname Il Braghettone for his work on a painting of this scene.
Hugo van der Goes and Hans Memling were both considered part of the early Netherlandish painters from this Dutch-speaking region of Belgium.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Gaspar Ofhuys' account of this painter's mental breakdown in which he thought his soul was lost inspired an Emile Wauters painting in which he sits on a monastery chair. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this painter and resident of the Red Cloisters, who painted a work whose off-kilter shepherds inspired Domenico Ghirlandaio's "Adoration of the Shepherds."

The Portinari altarpiece was the first oil on panel work in Florence, beating out Hans Memling's stolen painting of this scene. Daniele da Voltera earned the nickname Il Braghettone for his work on a painting of this scene.
Hugo van der Goes and Hans Memling were both considered part of the early Netherlandish painters from this Dutch-speaking region of Belgium.

bonuses.leadin: A female fifteenth century artist depicted the Maid of this city petting a lion for its flag. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this city, which Agnes van den Bossche painted in. Two brothers painted an altarpiece in this city that depicts the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb in its lower register.

Hans Memling featured van der Bossche’s Maid of Ghent as a background figure of the Moreel Family in one of these artworks. These artworks contain a central scene with two foldable wings and are frequently used as altarpieces.

van der Bossche studied in the painter’s guild of Ghent under this artist. Domenico Ghirlandaio’s depiction of the Adoration of the Shepherds in the Sassetti Chapel was inspired by viewing this artist’s Portinari Altarpiece, which was the largest Flemish work in Florence.
answers: Hugo van der Goes [or Hugo of Antwerp; or Ugo d’Anversa]
answers_sanitized: Ghent [or Gaunt; or The Ghent Altarpiece; or Maid of Ghent]
Hugo van der Goes [or Hugo of Antwerp; or Ugo d'Anversa]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A female fifteenth century artist depicted the Maid of this city petting a lion for its flag. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this city, which Agnes van den Bossche painted in. Two brothers painted an altarpiece in this city that depicts the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb in its lower register.

Hans Memling featured van der Bossche's Maid of Ghent as a background figure of the Moreel Family in one of these artworks. These artworks contain a central scene with two foldable wings and are frequently used as altarpieces.

van der Bossche studied in the painter's guild of Ghent under this artist. Domenico Ghirlandaio's depiction of the Adoration of the Shepherds in the Sassetti Chapel was inspired by viewing this artist's Portinari Altarpiece, which was the largest Flemish work in Florence.

bonuses.leadin: The best-known painting by the Belgian artist Emile Wauters depicts this artist’s late-career mental breakdown, which van Gogh compared himself to in letters to his brother. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist, who depicted the Adoration of the Magi in his Monforte Altarpiece. His best-known painting was installed in the hospital of Santa Maria Nuova, where it became a major influence on Italian artists.

van der Goes was said to have gone mad after his Portinari Altarpiece failed to exceed the Ghent Altarpiece, a work by two artists with this surname. One of those artists with this surname painted the Arnolfini Marriage.

The Portinari Altarpiece displays this scene in grisaille [griz-EYE] on its exterior panels, as does the Ghent Altarpiece. Depictions of this scene, like the one in the centre of the Merode Altarpiece, often include lilies.
answers: van Eyck [accept Jan van Eyck or Hubert van Eyck; prompt on partial surname]
The Annunciation to the Blessed Virgin Mary
answers_sanitized: van Eyck [accept Jan van Eyck or Hubert van Eyck; prompt on partial surname]
The Annunciation to the Blessed Virgin Mary

van der Goes was said to have gone mad after his Portinari Altarpiece failed to exceed the Ghent Altarpiece, a work by two artists with this surname. One of those artists with this surname painted the Arnolfini Marriage.

The Portinari Altarpiece displays this scene in grisaille [griz-EYE] on its exterior panels, as does the Ghent Altarpiece. Depictions of this scene, like the one in the centre of the Merode Altarpiece, often include lilies.

bonuses.leadin: Name some Early Netherlandish painters, for 10 points each.
parts: With his brother Hubert, this artist created the Ghent Altarpiece. He also painted The Arnolfini Wedding.

This artist’s version of The Descent from the Cross shows a skull below a fainting Mary and puts Jesus in a T-shape pose. He painted the birth, death, and resurrection of Jesus in the Miraflores Altarpiece.

This artist created a richly colored depiction of the Adoration of the Magi called the Monforte Altarpiece. Some credited Andrea del Castagno with creating this artist’s famous depiction of the Adoration of the Shepherds.
answers: Hugo van der Goes [or Hugo of Antwerp; or Ugo d’Anversa; prompt on Hugo]
answers_sanitized: Rogiervan der Weyden [accept either underlined portion]
Hugo van der Goes [or Hugo of Antwerp; or Ugo d'Anversa; prompt on Hugo]

This artist created a richly colored depiction of the Adoration of the Magi called the Monforte Altarpiece. Some credited Andrea del Castagno with creating this artist's famous depiction of the Adoration of the Shepherds.
